title: NOC 40040 – Commissioned Police Officers and Related Occupations in Public Protection Services | GTR Immigration
description: Commissioned Police Officers and Related Occupations in Public Protection Services under NOC 40040 are senior law enforcement leaders responsible for directing police forces and overseeing public safety operations across Canada. Classified as TEER 0 (Management Occupation), this role represents one of the highest-ranking leadership positions within Canada’s public protection sector. With ongoing investments in public safety, border security, and criminal justice reform, experienced commissioned officers remain essential to Canada’s law enforcement infrastructure.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group40 – Managers in public administration and public protection servicesSub-Major Group400 – Managers in public administration and protection servicesMinor Group4004 – Managers in public protection servicesNOC Code40040

duties: Commissioned Police Officers perform some or all of the following:
Plan, organize, direct, and evaluate police force operations
Develop and implement law enforcement policies
Ensure compliance with federal and provincial legislation
Coordinate departmental budgets and resource allocation
Represent the police service in public and governmental forums
employment_requirements: University degree in social sciences, criminal justice, or business administration (usually required)
Several years of experience as a police officer
Most senior roles require Canadian citizenship and successful completion of Canadian police academy and service requirements.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $75,100 to $166,600, depending on rank and jurisdiction.
The outlook for NOC 40040 is stable, supported by:
Advancement into commissioned ranks typically follows extensive service within Canadian law enforcement agencies.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 40040 are employed in:
Key opportunities exist within major provinces such as Ontario and federal agencies like the Royal Canadian Mounted Police.
Strong pension and public service benefits
Leadership roles within Canada’s criminal justice system
However, it is important to note that most commissioned officer positions require prior Canadian law enforcement service and citizenship eligibility.
